company.name: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Incorporated
company.subtitle: Total Revenues $12B+ 2025 | CF franchise (Trikafta+Alyftrek) $11.2B + Casgevy + Journavx | Kidney franchise (povetacicept Ph3 IgAN POSITIVE Mar 2026) | T1D islet cells
company.cashRunway: Self-funding (~$4B+ op cash flow); post-Alpine $4.9B acq Apr 2024 (povetacicept)

assets.name: Trikafta/Kaftrio (elexacaftor/tezacaftor/ivacaftor) — CFTR triple combo
assets.stage: Commercial ($10.6B 2025; CF dominant; Alyftrek next-gen ramping)

indications.name: Cystic fibrosis F508del + responsive mutations (~95% CF eligible)

market.notes: CFTR triple combo (corrector + corrector + potentiator) — class-defining CF therapy. Label expanded to 95% of CF patients. Alyftrek (next-gen) cannibalizing as patients switch.

assets.name: Alyftrek (vanzacaftor/tezacaftor/deutivacaftor) — next-gen CFTR triple QD
assets.stage: Commercial ($650M 2025 ramp; FDA approval Dec 2024; Alyftrek-eligible mutation expansion 2025)

market.notes: Once-daily next-gen CFTR triple. Differentiated from Trikafta: QD dosing, lower royalty (no Crispr/Cystic Fibrosis Foundation milestone), better sweat chloride. Growing as patients switch from Trikafta.

assets.name: Symdeko/Symkevi + Orkambi + Kalydeco — legacy CFTR modulators
assets.stage: Commercial ($550M combined 2025; declining as Trikafta+Alyftrek dominate)

indications.name: Legacy CFTR modulators (older mutations + niche pediatric)

market.notes: Symdeko (teza/iva double, ~$200M), Orkambi (lumacaftor/iva, ~$100M), Kalydeco (ivacaftor mono for G551D + niche, ~$250M). All being cannibalized by Trikafta + Alyftrek.

assets.name: Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel) — CRISPR-Cas9 BCL11A gene editing (w/ CRSP)
assets.stage: Commercial ($116M 2025; first CRISPR-edited cell therapy; ATC capacity expanding)

market.notes: First CRISPR-Cas9 gene-edited cell therapy. Ex-vivo edit of BCL11A enhancer to reactivate fetal Hb. ~$2.2M one-time WAC. Apheresis + busulfan conditioning -- ATC capacity bottleneck. ~70% of brand. Splits 50/50 economic with CRSP.

assets.name: Journavx (suzetrigine, VX-548) — NaV1.8 selective inhibitor (acute pain)
assets.stage: Commercial ($60M 2025 launch; first non-opioid acute pain in 25+ years; chronic pain Ph3)

market.notes: First-in-class peripheral NaV1.8 inhibitor for acute pain (FDA approved Jan 2025). Differentiated from opioids (no addiction/respiratory depression) and NSAIDs (selective sodium channel). Correctly tagged cns.pain.acute (new authoritative area). Chronic pain (DPN, lumbosacral radiculopathy) Ph3 ongoing.

assets.name: Povetacicept (pove) — TACI-Fc fusion (BAFF + APRIL inhibitor, ex-Alpine acq $4.9B 2024)
assets.stage: Phase 3 RAINIER IgAN POSITIVE Mar 2026 (W36 interim); BLA target end Mar 2026

market.notes: TACI-Fc fusion blocking BAFF + APRIL (B-cell depleter). RAINIER W36 interim Mar 2026 positive: 77.4% Gd-IgA1 reduction + 85.1% hematuria resolution. BLA filing end Mar 2026 (priority voucher pending). Class peers: Tarpeyo (Calliditas budesonide), atrasentan (NVS), sparsentan (TVTX), Fabhalta (NVS factor B).

assets.name: Inaxaplin (VX-147) — APOL1 small-molecule inhibitor (APOL1-mediated CKD)
assets.stage: Phase 2/3 AMPLITUDE adaptive trial; interim cohort enrollment complete

market.notes: First-in-class APOL1 inhibitor. AMPLITUDE Ph2/3 adaptive. Targets ~13% of Black Americans (G1/G2 carriers); proteinuric kidney disease (FSGS + collapsing glomerulopathy + HIV-associated nephropathy phenotypes). Phase 2a -47.6% UPCR reduction (NEJM 2023).

assets.name: VX-880 + VX-264 — fully differentiated allogeneic islet cell therapy (T1D)
assets.stage: Phase 1/2 VX-880 (immunosuppressed) + VX-264 (encapsulated, no IS); cure-potential T1D

indications.name: Type 1 diabetes with severe hypoglycemic episodes

market.notes: Allogeneic stem-cell-derived pancreatic islet cells. VX-880 (IV with immunosuppression; Ph1/2 patients insulin-independent). VX-264 (encapsulated, no IS needed; earlier-stage). Cure-potential for T1D ~1.6M US.

assets.name: VX-407 — PC1 modulator (autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease)
assets.stage: Phase 2 ADPKD; differentiated vs Jynarque (Otsuka tolvaptan)

market.notes: Small-molecule PC1 modulator addressing root cause of ADPKD (kidney pathology dominant). Class peer: Jynarque (Otsuka tolvaptan V2 antagonist, hepatotoxicity REMS).

assets.name: Skyclarys (omaveloxolone) — Nrf2 activator (Friedreich ataxia, ex-Reata $7.2B 2023)
assets.stage: Commercial (~$165M 2025; first & only Friedreich ataxia drug)

market.notes: Nrf2 activator (anti-oxidative stress). Approved Feb 2023 for FA (16+ years; expanded to peds 2024). Reata $7.2B Aug 2023 acq. Class peers: gene therapy (Lexeo LX2006, ELONA) + RNA pipeline emerging.

assets.name: Other revenues (CF ROW alliance + CRISPR collab + license)

market.notes: Other product revenue (small CF ROW alliance), CRISPR collab amortization (Casgevy 50/50 with CRSP), Crispr Tx alliance milestones, Cystic Fibrosis Foundation legacy royalty. ~$200M.

mega_bear.narrative: Trikafta -> Alyftrek transition flat; Casgevy ATC bottleneck persists; Journavx slow ramp; povetacicept Ph3 readout disappoints
events: X CF franchise plateaus at $11B (no further label expansion)
X Casgevy ATC capacity caps at <\$300M peak
X Journavx limited adoption vs opioids/NSAIDs
X Povetacicept Ph3 confirmatory mixed
X Inaxaplin Ph2/3 disappoints

bear.narrative: CF franchise stable; Alyftrek converts 50% of Trikafta; povetacicept approved IgAN; Casgevy slow ramp
events: ~ CF franchise $11.5B peak with Alyftrek transition
~ Casgevy $400M peak ATC capacity-limited
~ Journavx $300M peak chronic pain
~ Povetacicept approved IgAN 2026; modest

base.narrative: CF $13B peak Alyftrek-led; Povetacicept IgAN+pMN $3B+ across kidney franchise; Journavx chronic pain expansion; Casgevy $1B
events: V CF franchise $13B+ peak with Alyftrek dominance + label expansions
V Povetacicept IgAN approved 2026; pMN + lupus + MG expansions $3B+
V Inaxaplin AMKD approved $2B peak (13% of Black Americans)
V Journavx chronic pain (DPN + radiculopathy) Ph3 positive
V Casgevy $1B+ peak ATC scaling
V VX-880 T1D Ph3 starts

bull.narrative: Kidney franchise $7B+; CF $14B; Casgevy $2B+; Journavx chronic pain $2B+; T1D islet cells transformational
events: V Povetacicept best-in-class anti-BAFF/APRIL across IgAN+pMN+lupus+MG -- $7B peak
V Inaxaplin AMKD genotype-driven $3B peak
V VX-880/VX-264 T1D islet cells Ph2/3 transformational data
V Casgevy $2B with manufacturing scale
V Journavx $2B+ chronic pain

psychedelic_bull.narrative: Multi-franchise dominance: CF $14B + Kidney $10B+ + Pain $4B + T1D islet cures
events: V Povetacicept dominates B-cell-mediated diseases ($10B+)
V VX-880/VX-264 T1D effective cure transforms diabetes care
V Premium multiple expansion to peak biopharma

lastData.date: Feb 2026 (FY2025) + Annual Report 2025 + RAINIER readout Mar 9, 2026
lastData.title: FY2025 CF franchise dominant (~$11.2B); Casgevy + Journavx launching; povetacicept RAINIER Ph3 IgAN POSITIVE (Mar 9 2026 W36 interim) -- BLA target end Mar 2026; kidney franchise emerging.
points: Alyftrek FDA approval Dec 2024 (next-gen QD CFTR triple)
CF eligibility expanded to ~95% of patients (label expansion)
Casgevy $116M (CRISPR-edited cell therapy SCD + beta-thal; ATC capacity-bottlenecked)
Journavx $60M (FDA approval Jan 2025; first non-opioid acute pain in 25 years)
Povetacicept RAINIER Ph3 IgAN W36 interim POSITIVE (Mar 9 2026): 77.4% Gd-IgA1 reduction, 85.1% hematuria resolution
Povetacicept BLA target end Mar 2026 with priority voucher
Inaxaplin (VX-147 APOL1) AMPLITUDE Ph2/3 interim cohort enrollment complete
VX-880 / VX-264 T1D islet cells -- Ph1/2 patients insulin-independent
Reata $7.2B acq 2023 brought Skyclarys (Friedreich ataxia) -- separate franchise

nextCatalyst.title: Povetacicept BLA + IgAN PDUFA (priority voucher) + AMPLITUDE inaxaplin
thresholds: H2 2026: Povetacicept IgAN FDA approval (priority review)
2026-27: Povetacicept pMN Ph3 readouts + lupus + MG expansion
2027: VX-880 T1D islet cells Ph2/3 expanded cohort
2026-27: Journavx chronic pain (DPN + radiculopathy) Ph3 readouts

risks: CF franchise concentration: >90% of revenue from Trikafta+Alyftrek; ~95% market penetration ceiling
Casgevy $2.2M one-time price + busulfan conditioning + ATC capacity bottleneck
Journavx vs entrenched opioids/NSAIDs; chronic pain Ph3 binary
Povetacicept pMN + lupus + MG class peer pressure (Filspari TVTX, Tarpeyo Calliditas)
Inaxaplin APOL1 Phase 3 confirmatory pending; small population (~13% of Black Americans)
T1D islet cells: VX-880 requires lifelong immunosuppression; VX-264 encapsulation early-stage
Skyclarys (Friedreich ataxia, ex-Reata) acquisition synergy realization
Alpine $4.9B + Reata $7.2B + recent acquisitions -- goodwill impairment risk if pipeline disappoints

catalysts.title: Povetacicept BLA submission for IgAN (RAINIER Ph3 W36 interim positive Mar 9 2026)
catalysts._rationale: RAINIER W36 interim massively positive; priority voucher pending.

catalysts.title: Povetacicept FDA approval IgAN (priority review)
catalysts._rationale: Following BLA + priority voucher; standard 6-month review.

catalysts.title: Inaxaplin AMPLITUDE Ph2/3 interim cohort readout
catalysts._rationale: First APOL1-mediated CKD therapy; Ph2a -47.6% UPCR reduction supports.

catalysts.title: VX-880 T1D Ph2/3 expanded cohort data
catalysts._rationale: Ph1/2 patients have achieved insulin independence; cure-potential.
